verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (p24δ1, also known as TMP21) is a type-I single-pass transmembrane protein of the p24/TMED family. Its canonical residence is the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment (ERGIC) and cis-Golgi, where it functions in COPI/COPII vesicle biogenesis and cargo selection. However, TMED10 has a documented minority pool at the plasma membrane — surface biotinylation and proteomics studies detect it on the cell surface, and it has been reported as a cell-surface co-receptor for TGF-β signaling (TemporalS/TMP21 studies). Additionally, it has been identified as an entry factor for SARS-CoV-2, implying surface accessibility in at least some cell types. This dual localization (dominant Golgi/ERGIC pool + minority PM pool) places it in the contextual/dual_localization bucket rather than yes or no.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The size and biological significance of the PM pool vs. the dominant Golgi/ERGIC pool is not fully characterized; surface display may be cell-type-restricted or cycling-dependent.
